summary: Large nomenclature-only cleanup replacing ambiguous gm scene names using Major/Minor terminology with GameMode/GameScene naming across gm headers, gm source files, dependent menu/interface/library/toy files, symbols.txt, and table-typer tooling. Evidence shows matched/report output had no GALE01 changes and reviews were approving, while one reviewer noted the community had settled on the old Major/Minor terminology and future reconciliation may be needed.

what_changed.text: Renamed the top-level scene enum/type concept from MajorSceneKind/MajorScene/MJ_* to GameModeKind/GameMode/GM_* in the central gm forward header and across many use sites.
Renamed the lower-level scene enum/type concept from MinorSceneKind/MinorScene/MinorSceneHandler/MinorSceneInfo/MN_* to GameSceneKind/GameScene/GameSceneHandler/GameSceneInfo/GS_*.
Renamed scene setter symbols gm_SetSceneMinor and gm_SetScenePendingMinor to gm_SetScene and gm_SetPendingScene in symbols.txt.
Renamed many global data symbols ending in _MinorScenes to _Scenes while preserving their addresses, sizes, scopes, and section metadata in symbols.txt.
Applied the naming migration broadly across 79 changed files, mostly under src/melee/gm, with additional dependent updates in if, lb, mn, ty, and tools/table-typer.
Automated GALE01 report stated 'No changes' for base 0030f76 to head eb19533.

smart_moves.text: The cleanup changed names consistently at the central typedef/enum declarations, call sites, symbol metadata, and supporting tooling instead of only adding local aliases.
The symbols.txt edits preserved addresses and metadata while renaming labels, reducing risk that a nomenclature cleanup would become a data/split ownership change.
The author framed the PR as resolving ambiguity in 'Minor' and 'Major' terms, making the scope a naming cleanup rather than a behavioral rewrite.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For broad decomp nomenclature cleanups, update the owning forward/types headers, all dependent source/header users, symbols metadata, and any code-generation/table tooling together so the repository has one coherent vocabulary.
When renaming data symbols as part of a cleanup, preserve section address, object size, scope, and data annotations unless the PR is explicitly doing ownership or split work.
A nomenclature cleanup may be reviewable even when it conflicts with existing community shorthand, but the PR evidence should record that tension for later reconciliation.

naming_conventions.convention: Use GameModeKind and GM_* for the former MajorSceneKind/MJ_* enum values.
Use GameSceneKind and GS_* for the former MinorSceneKind/MN_* enum values.
Use GameMode, GameScene, GameSceneHandler, and GameSceneInfo for the former MajorScene, MinorScene, MinorSceneHandler, and MinorSceneInfo forward declarations.
Scene table data symbols in this PR use *_Scenes rather than *_MinorScenes.
Scene setter functions in this PR use gm_SetScene and gm_SetPendingScene rather than gm_SetSceneMinor and gm_SetScenePendingMinor.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: No assembly reshaping tactic is evidenced. The available automated report says GALE01 had no changes, consistent with a source/symbol nomenclature cleanup that did not affect generated output.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: GALE01 report for 0030f76 - eb19533: No changes.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.text: Looks good to me but I'll defer to @r-burns or @PsiLupan.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.text: LGTM; noted that the community had settled on Major/Minor terminology and that this difference may need to be reconciled eventually.

notes: The loaded diff is truncated, so individual edits in every changed file were not inspected; changed-file metadata confirms the full file list and line counts.
No inline review comments are present in the loaded evidence; review feedback comes from one approved review, one LGTM issue comment, and one bot report.
The PR body explicitly describes ambiguity in 'Minor' and 'Major' definitions, but the Discord conversation linked from the body was not loaded; no claims from that conversation are included.
The bot report says 'No changes' for GALE01, but no local build/objdiff command output beyond that report is loaded.
Merged state and timestamps are present in metadata/raw PR JSON, but acceptance as a global naming standard is not inferred beyond this PR.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Broad nomenclature cleanups should update central declarations, use sites, symbols, and tooling together
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2517 changed gm scene terminology coherently across forward declarations, enum constants, source/header call sites, symbols.txt, and tools/table-typer rather than introducing aliases or partial renames.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Preserve symbol metadata when a PR is only renaming scene/data labels
accepted_candidate_records.text: The symbols.txt excerpt renames many scene-related labels while leaving addresses, sizes, scopes, and data annotations unchanged, keeping the cleanup focused on nomenclature rather than data ownership.

source_update_candidates.title: gm scene nomenclature after PR #2517
source_update_candidates.text: In the loaded PR #2517 evidence, src/melee/gm/forward.h renames MajorSceneKind/MJ_* to GameModeKind/GM_* and MinorSceneKind/MN_* to GameSceneKind/GS_*. Related forward declarations become GameMode, GameScene, GameSceneHandler, and GameSceneInfo.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a path-scoped naming fact supported by the diff, but should be curated against current source before becoming a maintained path fact.

source_update_candidates.title: Scene table symbol suffix cleanup in config/GALE01/symbols.txt
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2517 renames many gm data symbols from *_MinorScenes to *_Scenes and gm_SetSceneMinor/gm_SetScenePendingMinor to gm_SetScene/gm_SetPendingScene while preserving address and metadata fields in the shown symbols.txt excerpt.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful as a source-owned symbol naming fact, but should be checked against current symbols.txt before promotion.

source_update_candidates.title: Record community-term tension during nomenclature cleanups
source_update_candidates.text: When replacing established community shorthand with clearer repository nomenclature, record both the ambiguity being fixed and any review note that the old term is community-settled, so future contributors can reconcile naming history without guessing intent.
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR provides a concrete example, but one PR is not enough to establish a global standard without curator review.

rejection_notes: Do not promote 'GameMode/GameScene is globally preferred everywhere' as an accepted standard from this intake alone; the evidence is specific to this merged gm scene cleanup and current source should be checked.
Do not infer behavior changes, matching improvements, or objdiff verification beyond the loaded bot report's 'No changes'.
Do not use the unloaded Discord conversation as evidence for naming intent or consensus.
Do not infer that the reviewer opposed the PR; the loaded PsiLupan comment says LGTM while noting community terminology tension.
